title: Add Architecture Linter with real-time anti-pattern detection
description: Surface existing topology-rules.ts (20+ rules) as inline warnings on nodes/edges. When user places a single database behind a high-traffic service, a red squiggle appears with tooltip: 'Single point of failure — no read replicas for this traffic level.' Reuse rule-database.ts for rule matching. Add LintOverlay component that maps violations to node positions. Debounce at 300ms.
acceptanceCriteria: Anti-patterns detected in real-time as user builds (debounced at 300ms)
Red underline or warning icon appears on violating nodes/edges with tooltip explanation
At least 10 rules active: SPOF, no cache, no LB, no monitoring, no auth, tight coupling, sync chains, missing backpressure, single region, no circuit breaker
Warnings dismissible per-instance without disabling the linter

title: Add Component Complaints — speech bubbles from stressed services during simulation
description: During simulation, when a component exceeds utilization thresholds or encounters issues, show a speech bubble near the node with a personality-driven complaint. Reuse narrative-engine.ts templates for text generation. Show max 2 bubbles at once, prioritized by severity. E.g., 'I'm the Redis cache and I keep getting evicted entries for hot keys. Maybe consider a dedicated hot-key cache?'
acceptanceCriteria: Speech bubbles appear near stressed components during active simulation
Messages are educational (explain the problem AND suggest a fix)
Maximum 2 bubbles visible simultaneously, prioritized by severity
Bubbles auto-dismiss after 8 seconds or on user click
Can be toggled off in settings

title: Add Smart-Connect auto-suggest edges when dragging near compatible nodes
description: When user drags a node within 150px of a compatible node, show a ghost dashed line suggesting the connection with an edge type label (e.g., 'db-query' between API server and database). User clicks to confirm or ignores. Use topology-rules.ts to determine valid connection types. Ghost edge styled as dashed with 40% opacity.
acceptanceCriteria: Ghost edge appears when dragging a node near a compatible node
Edge type is contextually correct (db-query for database, cache-lookup for cache, etc.)
Single click confirms the connection; moving away dismisses it
No ghost edges between incompatible pairs (e.g., two databases)

title: Add Panic/Cram Mode — stripped skeleton view for interview prep
description: Toggle that strips all decoration: stops animations, hides particles/heatmaps, shows skeleton topology with only essential labels, adds a side panel 'cheat sheet' with 5 key points per template. Targets users 24 hours before an interview who need distilled, focused content.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle accessible from toolbar or Cmd+Shift+P
All animations, particles, and decorative elements hidden
Skeleton view shows only: nodes as simple rectangles, labels, connection lines
Cheat sheet panel shows 5 key talking points for the current template

title: Add Component Micro-Lessons — 30-second popup explainer on first drag
description: When a user drags a component type for the first time, show a 30-second inline popup explaining: what it does, when to use it, common mistakes. Content from existing explanation-mode.ts. Only shows once per component type (persisted in localStorage). Dismissible with 'Got it' button.
acceptanceCriteria: Popup appears on first drag of each component type (not on subsequent drags)
Content includes: 1-sentence description, 'Use when:' bullet, 'Common mistake:' bullet
Dismissal state persisted in localStorage
Can be re-enabled from settings for review

title: Add Requirements-First workflow that defines constraints before canvas unlocks
description: Optional mode where starting a new design shows a guided questionnaire: 'What are we building?', target users, read/write ratio, latency target, availability requirement. Answers populate canvas metadata (traffic config, SLA targets). Wire into existing estimation pad. Canvas unlocks after requirements defined. Teaches the #1 interview skill: clarifying requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Modal appears when starting new design in requirements-first mode
5-question form: system type, user scale, read/write ratio, latency target, availability SLA
Answers auto-populate simulation traffic config and SLA dashboard
Mode is optional (toggle in settings), not forced
Answers visible in a 'Requirements' panel during design

title: Add 'Prove It' mode — manually route requests through architecture for scoring
description: Overlay mode where a request appears at the entry point. User clicks nodes in sequence to trace the expected path. System compares against simulation engine's actual routing and scores accuracy. Shows diff: 'You missed the CDN layer for static assets' or 'Correct! This is the optimal path.' Teaches request-flow reasoning.
acceptanceCriteria: Request dot appears at entry node with prompt 'Click the path this request takes'
User clicks nodes in sequence; each click highlights the path segment
On completion, system scores: correct hops, missed hops, unnecessary hops
Visual diff shows user's path vs optimal path
Works with any loaded template or custom design

title: Add 'Fix the Expert's Mistake' challenge mode
description: Load a 'senior engineer's' architecture that has 2-3 subtle bugs (not obvious antipatterns — realistic mistakes). E.g., a cache too large causing GC pauses, a circuit breaker threshold too low, no message ordering guarantee in a chat system. User finds and fixes defects, gets scored. 5 initial challenges. Teaches architecture review skills.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 challenge scenarios available with 2-3 subtle bugs each
Bugs are realistic (not obvious SPOFs but nuanced config/design issues)
User modifications tracked and compared against solution set
Scoring shows: bugs found, bugs missed, false positives
Each challenge has explanation of why each bug matters

title: Add System Design Scales — 2-minute focused micro-drills on single concepts
description: Isolated exercises on single concepts: 'Add caching to this read-heavy API (2 min)', 'Configure rate limiting for this payment endpoint (2 min)', 'Add monitoring to this blind system (2 min)'. Each is a partial canvas + 1-2 requirements + timer + auto-score. Reuse existing TimeAttackMode infrastructure. 10 initial drills.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 micro-drills available, each focused on one concept
Each drill has: partial canvas, clear requirement, 2-minute timer
Auto-scoring on completion (percentage-based)
Accessible from main menu as 'Daily Practice' or 'Scales'
Results tracked for progress visualization

title: Add Requirements Drift — constraints change mid-simulation forcing adaptation
description: During a design challenge, requirements change mid-simulation via notification: 'Product just decided we need analytics on every redirect' or 'Traffic just 10x'd — the startup went viral on HN.' User must adapt their running design without downtime. Teaches adaptability — the #1 real interview skill. 5 drift events per challenge.
acceptanceCriteria: Drift events trigger mid-simulation as toast notifications
User has limited time to adapt (configurable, default 60 seconds)
System validates that the new requirement is met after adaptation
5 drift event types: traffic spike, new feature requirement, region expansion, compliance mandate, cost cut
Scoring accounts for adaptation speed and design quality

title: Add Break the System competitive challenges
description: User receives a healthy architecture and must crash it as fast as possible using chaos injection. Scored on: time to failure, number of events used, creativity (novel attack vectors). Leaderboard for fastest crashes. Teaches failure modes viscerally through destruction rather than construction. Reuses existing chaos engine.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 'break it' challenges with healthy architectures of varying complexity
Timer starts when user begins injecting chaos events
System detects failure (error rate > threshold or total cascade)
Score: time-to-failure, events used, severity of cascade
Leaderboard persisted in localStorage

title: Add Architecture Sonification — hear system health as ambient soundscape
description: Toggle audio during simulation. Map 5 simulation metrics to Web Audio API parameters: throughput→tempo, latency→pitch, error rate→distortion, utilization→volume, queue depth→reverb. Healthy system sounds like calm ambient music. Overloaded system sounds strained. Crash sounds dissonant. Reuse existing sound-engine.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio toggle in simulation controls (default off)
5 metrics mapped to distinct audio parameters
Healthy system produces pleasant ambient sound
Degradation audibly detectable before visual metrics show it
Respects prefers-reduced-motion by defaulting to off

title: Add Time Travel Paradox — change one past decision, see ripple effects
description: Extend existing time-travel scrubber: at any scrubbed frame, allow a what-if modification (e.g., change cache from LRU to LFU, swap database type, add/remove a component). Re-simulate from that point. Show split-screen comparison: original timeline vs modified timeline. Combine time-travel.ts + what-if-engine.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: User can pause simulation at any point and modify the architecture
Modified architecture re-simulates from that point forward
Split-screen shows original vs modified timelines with metrics comparison
Delta indicators show which metrics improved/worsened
Can branch multiple times from different points

title: Add Zoom to the Metal — double-click any component to see internals
description: Double-click a node to expand it into a detailed sub-canvas showing internal data structures. PostgreSQL→B-tree indexes, WAL, buffer pool, query planner. Redis→hash table, skip list, RDB/AOF persistence. Load Balancer→connection pool, health check, routing algorithm. Reuse existing data-structure visualizers (btree-viz, lsm-tree, bloom-filter, wal). 5 initial deep-dives.
acceptanceCriteria: Double-click any supported component opens a detail drawer/modal
5 components with internals: Database, Cache, Load Balancer, Message Queue, API Server
Each internal view is interactive (not just a static diagram)
Clear 'back' navigation to return to system-level view
Internals react to simulation state (e.g., buffer pool fill level matches load)

title: Add 'Spend the Cloud Budget' Neal.fun-style micro-toy
description: Standalone single-page experience: user gets a budget ($100M, $10M, $1M, or $100K) and drag sliders to allocate across: compute, storage, bandwidth, CDN, databases, caching, monitoring, security. Real-time visualization shows what architecture they can afford. Reuse existing cost-model.ts for pricing. Shareable via URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Standalone page accessible without account
4 budget tiers with different constraints
Sliders update architecture preview in real-time
Cost breakdown shows per-category spend
Shareable via URL encoding (existing to-url.ts)

title: Add Cadaver Lab — annotated real-world production architectures
description: 5 meticulously annotated architecture blueprints for famous systems: Netflix Video Pipeline, Uber Matching Service, Twitter Timeline, Slack Messaging, Discord Voice. Each loads as a full canvas with annotation overlays at key components explaining WHY each design decision was made. Click any component for deep explanation. Leverage existing blueprint template format.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 real-world architecture case studies available from main menu
Each has 10+ annotation points with detailed explanations
Annotations explain WHY (not just WHAT) each component exists
Simulation runnable on each to see behavior under load
Clearly labeled as 'inspired by' (not claiming to be exact replicas)

title: Add Architecture Evolution Time-Lapse — watch famous systems evolve
description: 3 animated architecture evolution replays: Netflix (2008 monolith → 2025 microservices), Twitter (early Ruby → 2023 recommendation engine), Uber (early MVP → multi-DC dispatch). Each is a sequence of canvas snapshots played back via existing time-travel scrubber. Narration at each evolution step explains what changed and why.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 evolution time-lapses available (Netflix, Twitter, Uber)
Each has 5-8 evolution stages with smooth transitions
Each stage has narration explaining what changed and why
User can pause, scrub, and explore at any stage
Stages are historically informed (not fabricated)

title: Add Architecture Cold Cases — true-crime style postmortem investigations
description: Narrative-driven challenge mode: 'The Case of the Cascading Failure: On November 3rd, a healthy microservice architecture suffered a 47-minute total outage starting from the payment service...' User investigates with evidence (logs, metrics snapshots, traces), interrogates components, identifies root cause. 5 initial cases covering different failure patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 investigation scenarios with narrative framing
Evidence panel with logs, metrics, and traces to examine
User can 'interrogate' components (click for state at time of incident)
Submit root cause analysis for scoring
Each case teaches a different failure pattern (cascade, split-brain, thundering herd, etc.)

title: Add Bidirectional Architecture DSL with split-pane code/canvas editor
description: Split-pane view: left is a code editor with syntax highlighting for a minimal architecture DSL, right is the canvas. Type 'lb -> api-server x3 -> postgres' and 3 API servers appear connected. Drag a component on canvas and the code updates. Define minimal DSL grammar (10-15 keywords). Use PEG parser. Existing JSON import/export as intermediate format.
acceptanceCriteria: Split pane with code editor (left) and canvas (right)
Code-to-canvas: typing DSL creates/updates nodes and edges
Canvas-to-code: dragging components updates the DSL text
DSL supports: component types, connections, replication (x3), grouping
Syntax highlighting and basic autocomplete in editor

title: Add Load Test Generator — export k6/Locust scripts from architecture
description: Generate load test scripts that match the user's architecture topology. Map API nodes to endpoints, use traffic config for virtual user counts, generate assertions from SLA targets. Template-based generation similar to terraform-exporter.ts pattern. Supports k6 (JavaScript) and Locust (Python).
acceptanceCriteria: Export button generates k6 script matching current architecture
Scripts include: endpoint definitions, traffic patterns, assertion thresholds
Traffic config (RPS, distribution) from simulation store used for VU counts
Generated script is runnable without modification (valid syntax)

title: Add Sharable Architecture Portfolio with public URL
description: Portfolio page listing all saved designs as cards with canvas thumbnails. 'Publish Portfolio' generates a shareable URL. Portfolio shows interactive architecture previews (not just screenshots). For job seekers to link on LinkedIn/resume. MVP: portfolio page listing localStorage designs, static HTML export with embedded snapshots, URL sharing via existing to-url.ts encoding.
acceptanceCriteria: Portfolio page shows grid of all saved designs with thumbnails
Each design card shows: name, component count, creation date
Publish generates a shareable URL that works without Architex account
Portfolio page is visually polished (suitable for resume/LinkedIn)

title: Add Flashcard Export — generate Anki-compatible cards from any canvas
description: Extract node types and their explanations from explanation-mode.ts. Generate flashcards: Q: 'Why use a message queue between API and worker?' A: 'Decouples processing, handles backpressure, enables retry on failure.' Export as CSV (Anki-importable) or JSON. Works from any template or custom design.
acceptanceCriteria: Export Flashcards button in toolbar/export menu
Cards generated for each unique component type on canvas
Q/A pairs include: what it does, when to use, common mistakes
CSV format compatible with Anki import
Minimum 3 cards per component type

title: Add ADHD-friendly 90-second micro-challenges with immediate feedback
description: Ultra-short challenges designed for attention spans that struggle with longer exercises. 90-second timer, one specific task (add caching, fix a SPOF, optimize a bottleneck), immediate score. Completion triggers a satisfying animation. 15 initial challenges covering different concepts. Reuse time-attack infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: 15 micro-challenges, each completable in 90 seconds
Each has one clear objective displayed prominently
Timer visible and creates gentle urgency (not anxiety)
Completion triggers celebratory animation + score
Daily rotation of 3 featured challenges
